frameworks.name: quasi-experimental causal inference
frameworks.one_line: pre-registered design exploits WWII bombing exposure as natural experiment

frameworks.name: genetically informed neuroimaging
frameworks.one_line: controls for genetic and environmental confounds in linking exposure to brain

frameworks.name: developmental origins of adult health
frameworks.one_line: prenatal stress shapes long-term neurodevelopmental and functional outcomes

frameworks.name: mediation analysis
frameworks.one_line: gray matter volume partially mediates exposure effects on cognition and obesity

core_question: Is in-utero exposure to WWII air raids associated with lasting differences in adult brain structure, cognition, and physical health?
key_assumptions: historical bombing records index acute prenatal stress exposure
genetic and environmental factors can be statistically controlled
trimester and distance to bombing define exposure dose
manipulations: quasi-experimental contrast across exposed vs unexposed individuals
key_findings: last-trimester exposure within 2 km associates with lower adult gray matter volume
gray matter reduction partially mediates adverse effects on intelligence
exposure also linked to higher obesity via mediated pathway
interpretations: prenatal acute stress plausibly causes long-term neurodevelopmental differences
fetal brain vulnerability highlights need to protect pregnant women in armed conflicts
